notes: Fix-first only pass, no roadmap items were listed this round. Issue 3 (Evening anchor card clipped by the fixed bottom tab bar): Screen's scroll ScrollView only ever added a flat theme.spacing.xxl (32px) of bottom content padding, with no awareness of the tab bar's actual height, so the last card in a long Home list ran into the fixed tab bar. Added an `extraBottomInset` prop to components/ui/Screen.tsx (added on top of the existing xxl padding in the scroll contentContainerStyle) and wired `useBottomTabBarHeight()` from @react-navigation/bottom-tabs into all three tab screens (Home, Reflect, Settings) so their scroll content always clears the real tab bar height on every platform, not just Home. Issues 1 (Reflect rendering identical content to Home) and 2 (Settings tab opening the paywall) were investigated against the current source and could not be reproduced in code: app/(tabs)/index.tsx, reflect.tsx, and settings.tsx are three fully distinct, fully built screens with no shadow/duplicate routes (confirmed via a full app/ directory listing), and app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx already carries a `screenListeners={{ tabPress: dismissModalsOnTabPress }}` handler (with an explanatory comment referencing this exact bug) that calls `router.dismissAll()` before every tab press specifically so an auto-triggered paywall modal never traps the Settings tab underneath it. This matches a fix already recorded in a prior round's report.json for the same symptom. Given the harness instruction not to run expo start/export, this was verified by static code/route inspection rather than a live capture; if the vision QA still reproduces either symptom on the next pass, it's most likely a timing/race artifact in how the capture tool taps tabs immediately after a paywall-triggering mount (e.g. Reflect's own first-visit hard-paywall effect) rather than a routing defect. `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean after all edits.
